title: CosMx RNA Slide Preparation
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"4tjll","text":"This protocol details the CosMx RNA slide preparation.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
materials_text: {"blocks":[{"key":"6qq1","text":" ","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":0,"length":1}],"data":{}},{"key":"9u38g","text":"3 ug/mL Proteinase K (CosMx FFPE slide preparation kit, RNA)","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"97t3m","text":"CosMx Human Universal Cell Characterization RNA Panel (CMX-H-USCP-1KP-R)","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"2lnr6","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"reagents","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"id":962810,"guid":"37875BBE075611EF9C050A58A9FEAC02","name":"Superfrost Plus Micro Slide","mol_weight":0,"sku":"48311-703","url":"https://us.vwr.com/store/product/4646061/null","cas_number":"","rrid":"","vendor":{"id":1914,"name":"VWR International (Avantor)","link":"https://www.vwr.com","is_requested":false,"image":{"source":"/img/vendors/placeholder.png","placeholder":"/img/vendors/placeholder.png"}},"is_public":true,"can_edit":false}}}}
steps: Samples were processed following the guidelines outlined in the CosMx slide preparation manual MAN-10159-02 for prostate cancer processed in Adelaide and MAN-10184-01-1 for all other tissues.

In summary, 5 µm sections were placed onto the 15 mm x 22 mm scannable area of a Superfrost Plus Micro Slide (VWR, 48311-703) and dried in an oven at 37 °C for 00:00:00 then kept at Room temperature with desiccant Overnight before storage at 4 °C .
The slides were then incubated Overnight at 60 °C for 17:00:00 - 18:00:00 and then brought to Room temperature for 00:03:00 - 00:05:00 .
This was followed by two xylene washes for 5 minutes each, and two washes in 100% ethanol for 2 minutes each. For normal tissues, use CitroSolv workflow in place of xylene.
The slides were washed in xylene for 00:05:00 (1/2).
The slides were washed in xylene for 00:05:00 (2/2).
The slides were washed in 100% ethanol for 00:02:00 (1/2).
The slides were washed in 100% ethanol for 00:02:00 (2/2).
The slides were then dried in an oven at 60 °C for 5 minutes.

The slides were immersed in pre-heated 1X Target Retrieval solution (CosMx FFPE slide preparation kit, RNA) in a pressure cooker (or steamer) at 100 °C for 00:15:00 , timing started once the retrieval solution reached 100 °C .
The slides were immediately transferred to water for 00:00:15 , then washed in 100% ethanol for 00:03:00 , and air-dried at Room temperature for 00:30:00 to 01:00:00 .

Incubation frames were attached to each slide, and a pre-warmed digestion buffer containing 3 μg/ml Proteinase K (CosMx FFPE slide preparation kit, RNA) and 1X PBS (AM9922, AM9625 was applied and incubated in a hybridization chamber for 00:30:00 .
The slides were then rinsed twice in water, and fiducials were applied at 0.001%, followed by a 00:05:00 incubation at Room temperature , shielded from light.
The slides underwent a 1X PBS wash for 00:01:00 , were fixed in 10% NBF for 00:01:00 , immediately followed by two washes in NBF stop buffer for 5 minutes each, and then a 00:05:00 wash in 1X PBS.
The slides were washed in NBF stop buffer for 00:05:00 (1/2).
The slides were washed in NBF stop buffer for 00:05:00 (2/2).
A 100 millimolar (mM) NHS-acetate solution was applied to the tissue for 00:15:00 at Room temperature , followed by two washes in 2X SSC for 5 minutes each.
Followed by two washes in 2X SSC for 00:05:00 (1/2).
Followed by two washes in 2X SSC for 00:05:00 (2/2).

The CosMx Human Universal Cell Characterization RNA Panel (CMX-H-USCP-1KP-R) targeting 950 human genes and a 50-target add-on panel set, were denatured at 95 °C for 00:02:00 and then cooled On ice for 00:01:00 .
A probe mix containing RNase inhibitor, buffer R, and nuclease free-water was applied to the slide and incubated for 17:00:00 in the hybridization chamber at 37 °C .
Post-incubation, the slides were washed twice in a final concentration of 50% deionized formamide and 2X SSC mix for 25 minutes each, followed by two washes in 2X SSC for 2 minutes each.
The slides were washed twice in a final concentration of 50% deionized formamide and 2X SSC mix for 00:25:00 (1/2).
The slides were washed twice in a final concentration of 50% deionized formamide and 2X SSC mix for 00:25:00 (2/2).
Followed by two washes in 2X SSC for 00:02:00 (1/2).
Followed by two washes in 2X SSC for 00:02:00 (2/2).
DAPI stock was diluted to 1:40 and applied to the slides for 00:15:00 at Room temperature , protected from light.

The slides were then washed in 1X PBS for 00:05:00 and stained for 01:00:00 with a cocktail of CD298/B2M, PanCK, CD45, and CD68 antibodies (Item # 121500020, 121500021, 121500022). Colon, HCC TMA, pancreas, and prostate cancer samples were stained with 4 µL of the CD298/B2M antibody, following the guidelines in MAN-10184-01-1.
In contrast, appendix, ileum, and prostate cancer samples received a staining of 8 µL of the CD298/B2M antibody, as specified in MAN-10159-01-1.
The slides were washed three times in 1X PBS for 5 minutes each and then stored in 2X SSC.
The slides were washed in 1X PBS for 00:05:00 and then stored in 2X SSC (1/3).
The slides were washed in 1X PBS for 00:05:00 and then stored in 2X SSC (2/3).
The slides were washed in 1X PBS for 00:05:00 and then stored in 2X SSC (3/3).

The pre-bleaching profile followed configuration C, while the cell segmentation profile adhered to configuration A, except for the HCC TMA, which used configuration E. These procedures were consistently applied across all sites: St. Jude Children's Research Hospital, Weill Cornell Medical Center, and the University of Adelaide, with fields of view (FOVs) remaining uniform across all three locations.
